top_signals.title: Indonesia Tightens Grip on Key Commodity Exports
top_signals.summary: Indonesia, the world’s biggest nickel producer and top coal exporter, is moving to tighten control over exports of key commodities, Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto said on Wednesday. The government is introducing a new regulation that require state-owned enterprises approved by the cabinet to be the sole exporters of several key commodities, beginning with coal, palm oil, and ferroalloys, Subianto told Parliament. “Today the Indonesian government that I lead will issue a regulation on management of commodity exports,” the…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.
